title: English – 3rd Grade (Year 1)
description: First year of English – 3rd grade Portugal – Aprendizagens Essenciais
descriptionPt: 1.º ano de Inglês – 3.º ano de escolaridade – Aprendizagens Essenciais

modules.title: Hello! All About Me
topics: Greetings: Hello, Hi, Good morning, Good afternoon, Good evening, Goodbye, Bye, See you
Introducing yourself: What's your name? My name is… / I'm… How old are you? I'm 8.
Feelings: How are you? I'm fine / happy / sad / tired / scared / angry / hungry / thirsty
Polite words: Please, Thank you, Sorry, You're welcome
The English alphabet: A to Z – spelling your name
objectives: Greet and say goodbye in English
Ask and answer How are you?
Recognise and say the 26 letters of the alphabet

modules.title: Numbers & Colours
topics: Numbers 1 to 20: one, two, three… twenty
Colours: red, blue, yellow, green, orange, purple, pink, black, white, brown, grey
Asking about colour: What colour is it? It's red. What's your favourite colour? My favourite colour is blue.
Counting objects: How many pencils? Three pencils. How many books? Five books.
Combining colours and numbers: I have got two red apples. There are three blue cars.
objectives: Ask and answer What colour is it?
Combine numbers and colours in short sentences

modules.title: My Classroom
topics: School objects: pencil, pen, rubber/eraser, ruler, sharpener, book, notebook, schoolbag, pencil case, scissors, glue, crayon
Classroom language: Open your book, Close the door, Sit down, Stand up, Listen, Look, Read, Write, Repeat, Put your hand up
What's this? It's a pencil. What are these? They're crayons.
Asking for help: Can I have a pencil, please? Here you are. Thank you.
Singular and plural: a book / two books, a pencil / three pencils

modules.title: My Family
topics: Family members: mother/mum, father/dad, sister, brother, grandmother/grandma, grandfather/grandpa, baby
Introducing family: This is my mum. Her name is… He is my brother.
Possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her – my mum, your dad, his sister, her brother
I have got / I haven't got: I have got a sister. I haven't got a brother. Have you got a pet?
Family descriptions: My mum is tall. My dad is funny. My sister is young.
objectives: Introduce family members using This is my…
Use my, your, his, her correctly
Use I have got / I haven't got for family

modules.title: Pets & Animals
topics: Pets: dog, cat, fish, bird, hamster, rabbit, turtle
Farm animals: cow, horse, pig, sheep, chicken, duck
Wild animals: lion, elephant, monkey, snake, bear, giraffe, crocodile
Describing animals: It's big / small / fast / slow. It's brown. It can run / swim / fly / jump.
Talking about pets: Have you got a pet? Yes, I have. I've got a dog. His name is Max.
objectives: Name common pets, farm and wild animals
Talk about your pet using have got

modules.title: My Body
topics: Head and face: head, hair, eyes, ears, nose, mouth, teeth, face
Body: shoulders, arms, hands, fingers, legs, knees, feet, toes, tummy
The senses: I can see with my eyes. I can hear with my ears. I can smell with my nose.
Instructions with body parts: Touch your nose! Clap your hands! Stamp your feet! Shake your head!
Describing yourself: I have got brown eyes and black hair. I have got two hands and ten fingers.
objectives: Name the main parts of the body and face
Use have got to describe physical features

modules.title: Food & Drinks
topics: Fruit: apple, banana, orange, grapes, strawberry, pear, watermelon
Food: bread, cheese, cake, pizza, rice, chicken, egg, sandwich, biscuit, ice cream, chocolate
Drinks: water, milk, juice, lemonade
Likes and dislikes: I like pizza. I don't like cheese. Do you like bananas? Yes, I do. / No, I don't.
Meals: breakfast, lunch, dinner – I have milk for breakfast.
objectives: Name common fruits, foods and drinks
Express likes and dislikes with I like / I don't like
Ask Do you like…? and answer
Say what you eat and drink

modules.title: Toys, Games & Playground
topics: Toys: ball, doll, teddy bear, car, train, robot, puzzle, bike, skateboard, kite
Playground: slide, swing, seesaw, climbing frame, sandpit
I like / I want: I like my bike. I want a robot. My favourite toy is my teddy bear.
Sharing: Can I play? Yes! Let's play together. It's my turn. It's your turn.
Colours and toys: I have got a red ball. She has got a blue doll.
objectives: Name common toys and playground equipment
Express preferences and wishes about toys
Use Let's and Can I…? for play
Describe toys using colours and adjectives

modules.title: Days of the Week & Seasons
topics: Days: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
Today, yesterday, tomorrow: What day is it today? Today is Monday.
Seasons: spring, summer, autumn, winter
Weather basics: sunny, rainy, cloudy, windy, hot, cold
Connecting seasons and weather: In summer it's hot and sunny. In winter it's cold and rainy.
objectives: Name all seven days of the week in order
Ask and answer What day is it today?

modules.title: I Can Do It!
topics: Action verbs: run, jump, swim, fly, sing, dance, draw, read, write, climb, ride a bike, play football
Can / Can't: I can swim. I can't fly. Can you dance? Yes, I can. / No, I can't.
Let's play! Imperatives: Run! Jump! Stop! Clap! Sing! Dance! Don't run!
Simon says: Simon says touch your head! Simon says jump!
Talking about abilities: I can read in English. She can sing. He can't ride a bike.
objectives: Express what you can and can't do

modules.title: Celebrations
topics: Christmas: Christmas tree, Santa Claus, present, star, stocking, Merry Christmas, reindeer
Halloween: pumpkin, witch, ghost, bat, spider, trick or treat, Happy Halloween
Easter: Easter egg, bunny, spring, flowers, Happy Easter
Birthday: Happy Birthday, cake, candles, balloons, present, party, How old are you?
Carnival: mask, costume, clown, music, dance
objectives: Learn key vocabulary for each celebration
Say Merry Christmas, Happy Birthday, etc.
Compare celebrations in Portugal and the UK

modules.title: My First Sentences
topics: I am / I'm: I'm 8. I'm happy. I'm Portuguese. I'm a boy / a girl.
I like / I don't like: I like football. I don't like spiders.
I have got / I haven't got: I have got a dog. I haven't got a cat.
I can / I can't: I can swim. I can't fly.
Questions and answers: What's your name? How old are you? What colour? How many? Do you like…?
objectives: Build sentences with I am, I like, I have got, I can
Use negative forms: don't like, haven't got, can't
Use English to communicate basic information
